camera: Inertia uses the camera so you can photograph a meal, send it for an AI-assisted calorie estimate, and review the result before saving it to your log.
photoLibrary: Inertia reads your library only when you choose a photo, so you can attach an existing meal picture to log or send for the same AI-assisted estimate.
biometrics: Inertia uses Face ID or Touch ID when you turn on the optional app lock in Settings, so your meal log and targets stay private on this device.
notifications: Inertia can send reminders and light nudges about your daily logging when you opt in from Settings—nothing is sent until you allow notifications.
appTracking: This allows the ad SDK to use the device advertising identifier for more relevant ads and basic measurement. You can decline; ads may still appear in a non-personalized form.
internet: Used to sign in, sync meals and profile with our servers, run meal estimates, load food images, and show ads when enabled.
vibrate: Short vibration for light feedback on some taps (no personal data).
adIdAndroid: When ads are enabled, Android may expose an advertising ID so ad networks can measure delivery and relevance, similar to the iOS tracking prompt.
